title: What Pretraining Teaches
concept: Next-token prediction forces the model to learn grammar, facts, reasoning, and context to minimize prediction error. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Data Curation & Filtering.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary task an AI performs during pretraining?

options.text: Predicting the next word in a text

exercises.explain: Next-token prediction is the core task that forces AI models to learn language structure and world knowledge.

cards.title: The Power of Guessing
cards.body: How does an AI learn about history, science, and grammar just by guessing the next word in a sentence? It sounds too simple, but this one goal forces the AI to build a massive map of human knowledge!

cards.title: What is Pretraining?
cards.body: Pretraining is the phase where an AI reads massive amounts of text and guesses the next word (token) over and over. To get good at this simple guessing game, the AI is forced to learn grammar, real-world facts, and even basic reasoning!

cards.title: The Text Adventure
cards.body: Think of next-token prediction like playing a text-adventure game. To guess the next room you walk into, you can't just guess random directions. You have to remember the map (facts), follow the path rules (grammar), and solve puzzles (logic) to make the right move!

cards.title: Learning Facts in Action
cards.body: If the AI sees: "The capital of France is...", it must predict "Paris". To make this guess correctly, the AI has to learn a geographical fact during its training process.

cards.title: More Than Just Spelling
cards.body: People often think pretraining only teaches AI how to spell. But spelling is just the surface! To guess the next word in a complex sentence, the AI must understand logic, emotions, and the relationships between ideas.

cards.title: Goal vs. Skill
cards.body: See how the simple goal of word prediction changes what the AI actually has to understand.

left.text: Predict the single next word in a sentence.

right.text: Understand grammar, geography, math, and logic to make that prediction.

exercises.prompt: How does next-token prediction force an AI to learn complex concepts?

options.text: By learning grammar, facts, and context to make better guesses.
By asking human teachers for the answers during training.
By ignoring the context and picking random words.

exercises.explain: To guess the next word correctly, the AI has to understand the context of the sentence, the rules of grammar, and the facts of the world.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The desire to predict the next word accurately is what drives the AI to learn logical reasoning.
exercises.explain: Yes! The goal of minimizing prediction error forces the AI to understand grammar, reasoning, and context.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to show what next-token prediction teaches the AI:
exercises.sentence: To predict the next word accurately, an AI must learn real-world [blank] and logic.
exercises.explain: To guess the next word in a sentence like "Water freezes at thirty-two degrees...", the AI has to learn real-world scientific facts.

exercises.prompt: Identify the method a model actually uses to learn during pretraining:

options.text: Using physical camera sensors to look at the world.
Reading billions of sentences to learn grammar and facts.
Browsing the live internet in real time to look up answers.

exercises.explain: AI models during pretraining learn from patterns in huge datasets of human text, not by browsing the live internet or using camera sensors.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following does an AI actually need to learn to get really good at next-token prediction?

options.text: Only basic spelling and punctuation rules.
Grammar, facts, logic, and context clues.
Nothing, because it just guesses completely random words.
How to search the live web in real time.

exercises.explain: By forcing the AI to guess the next word over and over, it is forced to learn grammar, real-world facts, and logical reasoning just to get better at the game. Next up: Data Curation & Filtering.
